sourceDisclaimer: All news verified from JAMB Official, MySchool.ng, and trusted Nigerian education sources. TechMed does not create news — we curate and verify.

items.headline: JAMB Sets 150 Minimum Cut-off for Universities
items.text: JAMB sets 150 minimum cut-off for universities, 100 for polytechnics — May 11, 2026
items.details: The 2026 JAMB Policy Meeting held on May 11, 2026 established the national minimum cut-off marks for all institutions. Universities cannot admit any candidate with a JAMB score below 150, while polytechnics and colleges of education have a minimum of 100. This is a binding policy — no institution can go below these minimums.
items.impact: If your JAMB score is below 150, you cannot gain admission to any university in 2026. Consider polytechnics, colleges of education, or preparing for JAMB 2027.
items.action: Check your JAMB score immediately. If below 150, explore alternative pathways.

items.headline: Public Universities Admission Deadline: October 31, 2026
items.text: Public universities must conclude admissions by October 31, 2026
items.details: All federal and state universities must complete their admission processes by October 31, 2026. This includes merit list, catchment, and ELDS (Educationally Less Developed States) admissions. No admissions can be processed after this date for the 2026/2027 academic session.
items.impact: Post-UTME and screening forms typically open June–August. First admission lists release September–October. You have a narrow window to act.
items.action: Monitor your chosen university's portal weekly. Do not miss the Post-UTME registration window.

items.headline: 4-Week Acceptance Window on CAPS
items.text: Candidates have 4 weeks to accept admission offers on CAPS or risk withdrawal
items.details: Once you receive an admission offer on JAMB CAPS, you have exactly 4 weeks (28 days) to accept it. If you fail to accept within this window, the offer is automatically withdrawn and may be given to another candidate. This is strictly enforced.
items.impact: Many students lose admission every year because they don't check CAPS regularly. Set a daily reminder to check your CAPS status.
items.action: Log into CAPS at https://caps.jamb.gov.ng today. Check your status and ensure your O'Level results are uploaded.

items.headline: UTME Mop-Up Examination: June 13, 2026
items.text: JAMB fixes June 13, 2026 for UTME mop-up examination
items.details: The 2026 UTME mop-up (resit) examination has been scheduled for June 13, 2026. This is for candidates who missed the main exam (April 16–25) due to technical issues, biometric failures, or other valid reasons approved by JAMB.
items.impact: If you were affected during the main exam, this is your final chance for 2026. No further resits will be offered.
items.action: Check your JAMB profile for mop-up eligibility. Print your exam slip immediately if approved.

items.headline: Direct Entry Registration Extended to May 22, 2026
items.text: JAMB extends Direct Entry registration deadline to May 22, 2026
items.details: JAMB has extended the 2026 Direct Entry (DE) registration deadline from the original date to May 22, 2026. This applies to candidates with ND, NCE, IJMB, JUPEB, or other A'Level qualifications seeking 200-level admission.
items.impact: If you have a diploma or A'Level result, Direct Entry lets you skip 100-level and enter as a sophomore. This is a faster path for qualified candidates.
items.action: Visit any accredited CBT center before May 22. Bring your qualification documents and create your DE profile.

items.headline: Minimum Admission Age Remains 16
items.text: Minimum admission age remains 16 — exceptions only under strict rules
items.details: The minimum age for university admission in Nigeria remains 16 years as of the 2026 policy meeting. Exceptions for gifted candidates under 16 require special approval from the university senate, JAMB, and the Ministry of Education — a rare and difficult process.
items.impact: If you are under 16, you are technically ineligible for most universities. Some institutions may not enforce this strictly, but federal universities are compliant.
items.action: If under 16, consider waiting or exploring institutions with flexible age policies. Do not falsify your age — JAMB verifies with NIMC.

items.headline: All Admissions Outside CAPS Are Invalid
items.text: All admissions outside CAPS are invalid — monitor your CAPS daily
items.details: JAMB has reiterated that any admission not processed through the Central Admission Processing System (CAPS) is null and void. Universities cannot offer admission through phone calls, SMS, WhatsApp, or in-person meetings alone. Every admission must be approved on CAPS.
items.impact: Scammers often claim to offer admission outside CAPS for a fee. This is fraud. Only trust admission offers visible on your CAPS dashboard.
items.action: Log into CAPS daily. If someone claims you have admission but CAPS shows nothing, it is a scam. Report to JAMB immediately.

items.headline: 2.24 Million Candidates Registered for JAMB 2026
items.text: 2.24 million candidates registered for JAMB 2026 UTME — 10.5% increase from 2025
items.details: A record 2.24 million candidates registered for the 2026 UTME, representing a 10.5% increase from 2025. Of these, 1.89 million sat for the exam (April 16–25, 2026). The highest score was 372/400, achieved by Owoeye Daniella of the TechMed ecosystem.
items.impact: More candidates = higher competition. Elite courses at top universities will see even higher cutoffs this year.
items.action: Your preparation must be exceptional. Use the TechMed predictor to know exactly where you stand.

items.headline: Education & Agriculture Candidates Exempt from UTME
items.text: Education & Agriculture (non-engineering) candidates exempt from UTME
items.details: Under the 2026 policy, candidates applying for Education courses (non-technical) and Agriculture (non-engineering) are exempt from sitting for UTME. They can gain admission through direct entry based on O'Level results alone, provided they meet the institution's requirements.
items.impact: If you are considering Education or Agriculture, you may not need JAMB at all. This opens a direct pathway to university admission.
items.action: Contact your chosen university's admissions office to confirm their policy on UTME exemption. Requirements vary by institution.

items.headline: Polytechnic & College of Education Deadlines
items.text: Polytechnics: November 30, 2026 | Colleges of Education: December 31, 2026
items.details: Polytechnics must conclude ND admissions by November 30, 2026. Colleges of Education have until December 31, 2026. These deadlines are more flexible than universities, giving candidates additional time to secure admission.
items.impact: If you miss university admission, polytechnics and colleges of education are excellent alternatives. You can later transfer or use your ND/NCE for Direct Entry.
items.action: Research polytechnics with strong programmes in your field. Many offer industry-relevant training with better job prospects.
